title: Slug Generator
shortDescription: Turn headings and phrases into clean URL slugs with lowercase text, hyphens, and no unwanted spaces.
metaDescription: Turn headings and phrases into clean URL slugs with lowercase text, hyphens, and no unwanted spaces.
intro: Convert text into a clean, lowercase URL slug with hyphens instead of spaces.
overview:
Use this tool to turn titles, headings, and short phrases into web-ready slugs that are easy to read and easy to place in a URL. It removes spacing issues and helps keep the result consistent when you want a neat path for a page, post, or category.

The output is useful when you want to standardize names from different sources. Paste the text, review the transformed slug, and compare a few versions if you need a shorter or more readable path before publishing.

It is also helpful for content planning and site cleanup, where the same naming rules need to be applied across many pages.
howItWorks: Paste or type the title, phrase, or sentence you want to transform.
The tool converts the text into a slug format with lowercase characters and hyphens.
Copy the result and adjust it if you want a shorter or more specific path.

useCases.title: Article title to permalink
useCases.description: Convert a blog headline such as “How to Prepare a Product Launch” into a structured path like how-to-prepare-a-product-launch.

useCases.title: Category page naming
useCases.description: Turn a category label such as “Winter Running Shoes” into a consistent URL segment like winter-running-shoes.

useCases.title: Product page URLs
useCases.description: Create readable product paths from names like “Stainless Steel Water Bottle 750 ml” for catalog pages.

useCases.title: Event and landing page paths
useCases.description: Transform campaign or event titles such as “Spring Webinar for Designers” into a neat slug for a landing page.

examples.title: Blog post title
examples.input: How to Start a Small Garden
examples.description: A title with spaces becomes a lowercase slug with hyphens between each word.

examples.title: Product name with numbers
examples.description: Numbers stay in place and the words are separated into a readable URL segment.

examples.title: Mixed punctuation
examples.description: Commas, symbols, and punctuation are removed so the result stays clean.

faq.question: What happens to capital letters and punctuation?
faq.answer: Capital letters are converted to lowercase, and punctuation such as commas, exclamation marks, and ampersands is removed from the final slug.

faq.question: Will accents and special characters stay in the slug?
faq.answer: Characters with accents may be normalized depending on the text rules used by the tool. If a letter has to be simplified, the output will show the standard URL-safe form.

faq.question: Why does my result have fewer words than my original text?
faq.answer: Short connecting words, symbols, or special characters may be removed to keep the slug clean. If you need every word preserved, shorten the source text carefully before converting it.

faq.question: Can I use the output exactly as it is in a URL?
faq.answer: Usually yes, but it is still a good idea to check whether your site has extra rules for dates, folders, or language-specific paths before publishing.
